column3: (Django renders CSRF tokens server-side; ZAP flags false positives on non-mutating pages and repeated forms)
(Server header is controlled by the runtime server or reverse proxy, not Django application code)
(Base64 disclosure findings are false positives for static assets and framework-generated values)
(Cacheability findings are informational for this local baseline target)
(Sec-Fetch request headers are browser-supplied client headers and not application response controls)
(Authentication form detection is expected on setup and login pages)
(Session management detection is expected for Django session flows)
(Setup form values are escaped by Django template rendering and covered by regression tests)
